overview: A minimal single-component web application that serves a static "Hello World" page to any visitor. The architecture consists of just one frontend web-app component with no backend dependencies.
requirements: Display 'Hello World' message on the main page
Accessible via any modern web browser
Expose a /health endpoint for liveness checks
Serve over HTTP on standard web port

components.name: hello-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello Web
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Static web app displaying Hello World
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Serve the Hello World page
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions: Create a minimal React + TypeScript app using Vite. The root page (/) should render a centered heading that says 'Hello World'. Build the static assets (npm run build) and serve them with nginx in the Docker image. Listen on port 80. Add an nginx location /health that returns 200 with JSON {"status":"ok"} and Content-Type application/json. Include a multi-stage Dockerfile: stage 1 node:20-alpine to build, stage 2 nginx:alpine to serve /usr/share/nginx/html. No backend or API calls required.
